lesson.menu_desc: Phone Expressions and Indirect Questions -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 8 -- Handling Phone Calls -- Phone Expressions and Indirect Questions
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Walyson Ginaldo Silva | Aula 8 -- Handling Phone Calls

listenings.text: Hi Walyson, this is Laura from the supplier office in Colombia. I am sorry I missed your call earlier; I was not available. I am calling about the samples for the Europe project. I was wondering if you could send me the final order by Friday. Could you also tell me how many bags you need this month? I tried to get through to your office twice, but the line was busy. If it is easier, you can call me back on extension three-one-five, or leave a message on my voicemail. I will be available all afternoon. Please do not worry about the small delay; the samples are ready, and I will send them on Monday. Thank you, and I hope to speak with you soon.

Hi David, this is Walyson from the trading desk. I am calling about the message we got from the supplier. She is not available right now, so I am leaving you a voicemail. Could you tell me when you will be back in the office? I was wondering if you could confirm the final order before Friday. I tried to put the supplier through to you earlier, but I could not get through. When you have a moment, please call me back on extension two-oh-four, or send me a quick message. Thank you, and talk soon.

extra_audio.text: First, answer the call and say your name and your company. Next, ask politely who is calling and how you can help. Then, if the person they want is not available, offer to take a message. After that, use an indirect question to check the important details. Finally, confirm the next step and say a polite goodbye.
